text:
I'm sorry you're having such a rough time right now. Terrible weeks happen to everyone, and the fact that you're reaching out—even just in this small way—shows real strength.

Here's what I want you to know: this week is temporary. The heaviness you're feeling right now won't last forever, even though it might feel that way in the moment. Bad weeks have a way of making everything feel permanent, but they're not. They're just weeks, and they end like all weeks do.

You've probably gotten through hard times before, even if they felt impossible at the time. You're still here, still trying, and that matters. The things that went wrong this week don't define you or your ability to have better days ahead. Sometimes life just piles on, and it's not fair, and it's not a reflection of who you are or what you deserve.

If you can, be gentle with yourself this weekend. Do something small that actually feels good, not because you should but because you want to. Rest if you need to. Talk to someone if that helps. Even small things count—a walk outside, time with someone who gets you, something that makes you laugh, whatever small kindness you can give yourself.

And if you need to let yourself feel the rough stuff for a bit longer, that's okay too. You don't have to be okay yet.

Next week is coming, and it will be a fresh start. But for now, just make it through. You're doing better than you think you are.
